title: We Cut 94% of AI Coding Tokens With a Local Code Index - Rajkumar Sakthivel, Tesco
summary: Tesco built a local code retrieval layer using AST-aware chunking, vector search combined with keyword matching, and lightweight relationship tracking to reduce tokens sent to AI coding agents by 94%. The system prioritizes context optimization over model tuning and includes LLM-based scoring with heuristic fallbacks to handle retrieval quality.
writeup: Rajkumar Sakthivel of Tesco describes a local code retrieval layer that cut tokens sent to AI coding agents by 94%. The approach combines AST-aware chunking, vector search paired with keyword matching, and lightweight relationship tracking, prioritizing context optimization over model tuning. Retrieval quality is managed through LLM-based scoring with heuristic fallbacks. The takeaway: shaping what context reaches the agent, rather than tuning the model, can sharply reduce token usage.
